sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check where the green player moved to
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'N':  # light green = player
        print(f"Player: id={n['id']} color={n['color']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'b':  # blue centers
        print(f"Blue target: id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'W':  # white block
        print(f"White block: id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player moved from (49,32)-(51,35) to (45,32)-(47,35). That's a jump of 4 rows up. The white block also moved from (48,32) to (44,32) - it seems like the player moved up past the white block, or the white block is part of the player's trail.
Wait, let me reconsider. The white block was at (48,32)-(48,35) originally, now it's at (44,32)-(44,35). The player was at (49,32)-(51,35), now at (45,32)-(47,35). So both moved up by 4 rows. This suggests the white block might be a "trail" or marker left behind.
Actually, looking more carefully: the player's boundary went from [[49, 32], [49, 35], [51, 35], [51, 32]] to [[45, 32], [45, 35], [47, 35], [47, 32]]. That's a movement of 4 cells up. And the white block went from [[48, 32], [48, 35]] to [[44, 32], [44, 35]] - also 4 cells up.
Hmm, actually the white block might have been at the old player position and now moved up. Or maybe the player leaves a white trail. Let me think...
Actually wait - the white block was at (48,32) originally, which is just above the player at (49,32). Now the white block is at (44,32) and the player is at (45,32). So the white block is still just above the player. It seems like the white block follows the player.
Let me try to navigate to one of the blue targets. The closest one seems to be the blue square at (37,33)-(38,34) which is inside the dark gray frame at (36,32)-(39,35). The player is at column 32-35, so it's aligned. Let me move up more to reach it.
Actually, let me first understand the movement better. The player moved 4 cells up with one UP press. Let me try more movements to reach the blue targets.
The player is at row 45-47, col 32-35. The blue square at (37,33)-(38,34) is within the dark gray frame at (36,32)-(39,35). If I move up, I should reach it.
Let me try moving up again to see if I can enter the frame.
